{"problem_standardized_math":"For the case of an elliptical trust region, verify that the generalized Cauchy point is obtained by taking \(p_k^c=\tau_k p_k^s\), where \(p_k^s\) is the solution of\n\\[\np_k^s = \\arg\\min_{p \\in \\mathbb{R}^n} \\; f_k + g_k^T p\n\\qquad \\text{subject to } \\|Dp\\| \\leq \\Delta_k,\n\\tag{4.58}\n\\]\nand that \(p_k^s\) and \(\tau_k\) are given by\n\\[\np_k^s = -\\frac{\\Delta_k}{\\|D^{-1}g_k\\|}D^{-2}g_k,\n\\tag{4.60}\n\\]\nand\n\\[\n\\tau_k =\n\\begin{cases}\n1, & \\text{if } g_k^T D^{-2} B_k D^{-2} g_k \\leq 0, \\\\[1ex]\n\\min\\left(\\dfrac{\\|D^{-1}g_k\\|^3}{\\Delta_k\\, g_k^T D^{-2} B_k D^{-2} g_k},\\, 1\\right), & \\text{otherwise.}\n\\end{cases}\n\\tag{4.61}\n\\]\nHint: using the theory of Chapter 12, one can show that the solution of (4.58) satisfies \(g_k + \\alpha D^2 p_k^s = 0\) for some scalar \(\\alpha \\ge 0\)."}
